message:
test(fuzz): add CBOR decoder recursion-limit boundary fuzz target (resolves 75nvg)

CanonicalSerializer::deserialize{,_unchecked} runs CBOR bytes through
ciborium's from_reader_with_recursion_limit, bounded by
MAX_DESERIALIZATION_RECURSION_LIMIT = 128 (lib.rs:433-466). This is the
decoder-side stack-overflow DoS guard — distinct from the encode-side
depth check that y98gt covers (Value-tree built MAX+10 deep). A
regression that dropped the explicit limit or fell back to ciborium's
default would silently re-open this surface for an attacker sending
10K-deep CBOR bytes.

Three properties:
- depth > MAX bytes MUST be rejected with a typed bounded-resource
  error (DepthExceeded / PayloadTooLarge / CborDeserialize /
  NonCanonicalEncoding) — never panic, never silently decode
- depth ≤ MAX bytes MUST decode (acceptance via deserialize_unchecked)
- mixed array+map alternating nesting at MAX+1 MUST be rejected
  symmetrically — guards depth-counting parity between Array and
  Map branches at canonicalize_value_in_place

Once-gated regression anchors:
- (a) depth=129 nested arrays MUST trip a depth-class error
- (b) depth=128 nested arrays MUST decode (acceptance, keeps the
  rejection anchor non-vacuous)
- (c) depth=129 nested maps MUST trip a depth-class error (parity
  guard against Array-only depth counting)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -525,5 +525,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_cbor_decode_recursion_limit"
+path = "fuzz_targets/cbor_decode_recursion_limit.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
